16.dv: a primitive root; properly to be complete, but used only as a denominative from H7651 (שֶׁבַע);

16.gl: to restrain or (reflex.) refrain; by implication, to refuse, spare, preserve; to observe
16.kj: assuage, [idiom] darken, forbear, hinder, hold back, keep (back), punish, refrain, reserve, spare, withhold.
16.dv: a primitive root; also interchangeable  with H2821 (חָשַׁךְ)

17.gl: to occupy (by driving out previous tenants, and possessing in their place); by implication, to seize, to rob, to inherit; also to expel, to impoverish, to ruin
17.kj: cast out, consume, destroy, disinherit, dispossess, drive(-ing) out, enjoy, expel, [idiom] without fail, (give to, leave for) inherit(-ance, -or) [phrase] magistrate, be (make) poo

17.gl: the lip (as a natural boundary); by implication, language; by analogy, a margin (of a vessel, water, cloth, etc.)
17.kj: band, bank, binding, border, brim, brink, edge, language, lip, prating, (sea-)shore, side, speech, talk, (vain) words.
17.dv: or (in dual and plural) שֶׂפֶת; probably from H5595 (סָפָה) or H8192 (שָׁפָה) through the idea of termination (compare H5490 (סוֹף));

17.gl: to kneel; by implication to bless God (as an act of adoration), and (vice-versa) man (as a benefit); also (by euphemism) to curse (God or the king, as treason)
17.kj: [idiom] abundantly, [idiom] altogether, [idiom] at all, blaspheme, bless, congratulate, curse, [idiom] greatly, [idiom] indeed, kneel (down), praise, salute, [idiom] still, thank.

17.gl: a sea (as breaking in noisy surf) or large body of water; specifically (with the article), the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river, or an artifical basin; locally, the west, or (rarely) the south
17.dv: from an unused root meaning to roar;

17.gl: the sky (as aloft; the dual perhaps alluding to the visible arch in which the clouds move, as well as to the higher ether where the celestial bodies revolve)
17.dv: dual of an unused singular שָׁמֶה; from an unused root meaning to be lofty;

18.dv: or קֹל; from an unused root meaning to call aloud;

18.gl: a heel, i.e. (figuratively) the last of anything (used adverbially, for ever); also result, i.e. compensation; and so (adverb with preposition or relatively) on account of
18.kj: [idiom] because, by, end, for, if, reward.
18.dv: from H6117 (עָקַב) in the sense of H6119 (עָקֵב);

18.gl: a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also (figuratively) a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts
18.dv: rarely (shortened) גֹּי; apparently from the same root as H1465 (גֵּוָה) (in the sense of massing);

18.gl: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
18.kj: [idiom] common, country, earth, field, ground, land, [idiom] natins, way, [phrase] wilderness, world.
18.dv: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm;

19.gl: Abraham, the later name of Abram
19.dv: contracted from H1 (אָב) and an unused root (probably meaning to be populous); father of a multitude;

21.gl: a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationship and metaphorical affinity or resemblance (like father))
21.kj: another, brother(-ly); kindred, like, other. Compare also the proper names beginning with 'Ah-' or 'Ahi-'.

21.gl: Aram or Syria, and its inhabitants; also the name of the son of Shem, a grandson of Nahor, and of an Israelite
21.dv: from the same as H759 (אַרְמוֹן); the highland;

21.gl: father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application
21.kj: chief, (fore-) father(-less), [idiom] patrimony, principal. Compare names in 'Abi-'.

21.gl: Kemuel, the name of a relative of Abraham, and of two Israelites
21.dv: from H6965 (קוּם) and H410 (אֵל); raised of God;

21.gl: Uts, a son of Aram, also a Seirite, and the regions settled by them.

22.gl: Bethuel, the name of a nephew of Abraham, and of a place in Palestine
22.dv: apparently from the same as H1326 (בָּתָה) and H410 (אֵל); destroyed of God;

23.gl: Abraham, the later name of Abram
23.dv: contracted from H1 (אָב) and an unused root (probably meaning to be populous); father of a multitude;

24.gl: an appellation, as amark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character
24.dv: a primitive word (perhaps rather from through the idea of definite and conspicuous position; compare H8064 (שָׁמַיִם));

24.gl: Maakah (or Maakath), the name of a place in Syria, also of a Mesopotamian, of three Israelites, and of four Israelitesses and one Syrian woman

24.gl: Tebach, the name of a Mesopotamian and of an Israelite

24.dv: feminine passive participle of H7213 (רָאַם); raised;
